canonical_definition: Based on the available data, interactive segmentation is defined as a task involving the segmentation of objects in an image through the active incorporation of user inputs. The operationalization of this behavior is central to its definition, with user inputs specified as points, boxes, or scribbles that guide the segmentation process. One source describes this as a method that "separates objects by actively integrating user inputs" ("Interfacing Foundation Models' Embeddings"). The task is also situated within the context of model training, where it is noted that training for interactive segmentation can occur alongside other tasks, such as image-text retrieval, without mutual interference.
papers: OMG-LLaVA: Bridging Image-level, Object-level, Pixel-level Reasoning and Understanding
VisionLLM v2: An End-to-End Generalist Multimodal Large Language Model for Hundreds of Vision-Language Tasks

definitions.text: The task of segmenting objects in an image by actively incorporating user inputs such as points, boxes, or scribbles.

The task is also situated within the context of model training, where it is noted that training for interactive segmentation can occur alongside other tasks, such as image-text retrieval, without mutual interference.
